ESP32-C3 Zero Protective Case

A 3D-printed classroom case designed in Tinkercad by Mohammed Alhendi.

The project developed a protective enclosure for the ESP32-C3 Zero board. The case protects the board during normal classroom handling while keeping access to the USB port, pins, buttons and ventilation.

PLASelected material
3.91 gFinal material use
20m49sFinal print estimate

Project Overview

Purpose

The case is made to protect the ESP32-C3 Zero from knocks, loose storage and normal classroom handling.

Usability

The design keeps clear access to important ports and pins so the board can still be connected and used.

Cooling

Side vents were added in the final design so the board is not fully enclosed without airflow.

Prototype Development

Prototype 1

The first version tested a simple two-piece enclosure. It was fast and low-cost to print, but it needed better support posts, access openings and ventilation.

Material2.98 g
Estimated time18m22s
Main improvement neededAccess, board support and airflow

Prototype 2

The second version added internal support posts and larger access cut-outs. It improved usability but still needed stronger ventilation and wall refinement.

Material3.21 g
Estimated time19m54s
Main improvement neededVent placement and wall strength

Prototype 3

The third version refined the shape and access points before the final design. It helped compare material use and decide what needed to be adjusted for the final model.

Material4.03 g
Estimated time20m49s
Main improvement neededFinal ventilation and finish

Final Design

The final case keeps the useful support and access ideas from the earlier prototypes, then adds side ventilation holes and a cleaner enclosure shape. The design is suitable for a classroom FDM printer and keeps the print small, light and practical.

Design softwareTinkercad
Printer typeClassroom FDM 3D printer
MaterialPLA filament
Final material3.91 g
Final estimated time20m49s
